name: Pub/Sub System with Hierarchical Topics
description: Implement a hierarchical topic-based publish/subscribe system across two modules. Module pubsub_types.py must define: Message dataclass with fields topic (str), payload (Any), id (int); SubscriberInfo dataclass with fields topic_pattern (str), handler (callable Message->None), id (int). Module pubsub_broker.py must import from pubsub_types.py and implement Broker class with methods: publish(topic: str, payload) returns assigned message id; subscribe(topic_pattern: str, handler) returns subscription id; unsubscribe(sub_id: int) -> bool; topics() returns sorted list of distinct topic strings ever published. Topic patterns use '.' as separator and '*' as single-level wildcard, '#' as multi-level wildcard. Examples: 'app.user.*' matches 'app.user.created' but not 'app.user.profile.updated'; 'app.#' matches 'app.x' and 'app.x.y'; 'app.*.created' matches 'app.user.created'.
signature:
# pubsub_types.py
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Any, Callable

@dataclass
class Message:
    topic: str
    payload: Any
    id: int

@dataclass
class SubscriberInfo:
    topic_pattern: str
    handler: Callable
    id: int

# pubsub_broker.py
from pubsub_types import Message, SubscriberInfo

class Broker:
    def __init__(self): ...
    def publish(self, topic: str, payload) -> int: ...
    def subscribe(self, topic_pattern: str, handler) -> int: ...
    def unsubscribe(self, sub_id: int) -> bool: ...
    def topics(self) -> list[str]: ...
tests:
def test_basic_pub_sub():
    b = Broker()
    received = []
    b.subscribe('app.user.created', lambda m: received.append(m.payload))
    b.publish('app.user.created', {'id': 1})
    assert received == [{'id': 1}]

def test_no_match_no_call():
    b = Broker()
    received = []
    b.subscribe('a.b', lambda m: received.append(m))
    b.publish('a.c', None)
    assert received == []

def test_single_wildcard():
    b = Broker()
    received = []
    b.subscribe('app.user.*', lambda m: received.append(m.topic))
    b.publish('app.user.created', None)
    b.publish('app.user.deleted', None)
    b.publish('app.user.profile.updated', None)
    assert sorted(received) == ['app.user.created', 'app.user.deleted']

def test_multi_wildcard():
    b = Broker()
    received = []
    b.subscribe('app.#', lambda m: received.append(m.topic))
    b.publish('app.x', None)
    b.publish('app.x.y', None)
    b.publish('app.x.y.z', None)
    b.publish('other.x', None)
    assert sorted(received) == ['app.x', 'app.x.y', 'app.x.y.z']

def test_middle_wildcard():
    b = Broker()
    received = []
    b.subscribe('app.*.created', lambda m: received.append(m.topic))
    b.publish('app.user.created', None)
    b.publish('app.order.created', None)
    b.publish('app.user.deleted', None)
    assert sorted(received) == ['app.order.created', 'app.user.created']

def test_multiple_subscribers():
    b = Broker()
    a, c = [], []
    b.subscribe('event', lambda m: a.append(1))
    b.subscribe('event', lambda m: c.append(1))
    b.publish('event', None)
    assert len(a) == 1 and len(c) == 1

def test_unsubscribe():
    b = Broker()
    received = []
    sid = b.subscribe('topic', lambda m: received.append(m))
    assert b.unsubscribe(sid) is True
    b.publish('topic', None)
    assert received == []

def test_message_id_increments():
    b = Broker()
    a = b.publish('t', 1)
    c = b.publish('t', 2)
    assert c > a

def test_topics_list():
    b = Broker()
    b.publish('a.b', 1)
    b.publish('c.d', 2)
    b.publish('a.b', 3)
    assert b.topics() == ['a.b', 'c.d']

def test_message_object_passed():
    b = Broker()
    captured = []
    b.subscribe('e', lambda m: captured.append(m))
    b.publish('e', 'hello')
    assert isinstance(captured[0], Message)
    assert captured[0].topic == 'e' and captured[0].payload == 'hello'
constraints: '#' wildcard matches one or more segments at the end of pattern
Subscription IDs and message IDs are unique within the broker
topics() returns distinct topics actually published, sorted ascending
Handlers are called synchronously in subscription order
